turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Michael,     ​Looks like you have initially used the the PQOH Order functionality to place few Orders, and then using the Production orders feature to place the remaining orders.      ​But its a recommendation from the development is to not use both functionalities together because the system is not designed to handle them both hand in hand yet. you can chose to use any of them but continue using only that until we have the system handle the usage of both.      When you use the  PQOH Order functionality to place Orders, it will lock the Production date range with the Last date until which the Plans are already in PLAN COMPLETE status, and then you wont be able to use that date range in Production order anymore, but you could use PQOH order to place the remaining orders any time.   ​   For now, we have unlocked the Production date range for you to be able to place the production orders as needed, but it is still recommended to use only one of the functionalities to avoid such issues in the future.   ​   ​   ​​   ​         ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

turns.body_html:
Hello Michael,

​Looks like you have initially used the the PQOH Order functionality to place few Orders, and then using the Production orders feature to place the remaining orders. 

​But its a recommendation from the development is to not use both functionalities together because the system is not designed to handle them both hand in hand yet. you can chose to use any of them but continue using only that until we have the system handle the usage of both. 

When you use the  PQOH Order functionality to place Orders, it will lock the Production date range with the Last date until which the Plans are already in PLAN COMPLETE status, and then you wont be able to use that date range in Production order anymore, but you could use PQOH order to place the remaining orders any time. 
​
For now, we have unlocked the Production date range for you to be able to place the production orders as needed, but it is still recommended to use only one of the functionalities to avoid such issues in the future. 
​
​
​​
​

turns.body_text:
Please take a look at ticket #292372 raised by Wil Turcios (wturcios@houstonisd.org).        Hi Sai/ Michael,      The district reported an issue with the Production orders at 1 of their sites not populating all the items as expected in the production order based of their Production Plans.      1.The site is "Project Chrysalis Middle school" and the food prep site is "Cage Elementary". However, most of the items on the plan are NOT Prep-Site Items.   ​2. I have verified the production plans are in Plan complete status.   ​3. Verified the Ingredients and Stock items are linked, and Food Required Qty populates the Stock items as expected on the production plans.   4. The Stock items have Valid Contract dates and the Vendor Item price dates, and are configured as orderable from the vendor.  5. Vendor has the site added on the Delivery schedule.      But still, not all items are populating on the production order.      Please let us know when you are available to screenshare with the customer, I can arrange for the meeting.              Example item :              Thank you,  Alekya Kotta | CYBERSOFT  Expert Care Team

turns.body_html:
Please take a look at ticket #292372 raised by Wil Turcios (wturcios@houstonisd.org).

Hi Sai/ Michael, 

The district reported an issue with the Production orders at 1 of their sites not populating all the items as expected in the production order based of their Production Plans. 

1.The site is "Project Chrysalis Middle school" and the food prep site is "Cage Elementary". However, most of the items on the plan are NOT Prep-Site Items. 
​2. I have verified the production plans are in Plan complete status. 
​3. Verified the Ingredients and Stock items are linked, and Food Required Qty populates the Stock items as expected on the production plans. 
4. The Stock items have Valid Contract dates and the Vendor Item price dates, and are configured as orderable from the vendor.
5. Vendor has the site added on the Delivery schedule. 

But still, not all items are populating on the production order. 

Please let us know when you are available to screenshare with the customer, I can arrange for the meeting. 



Example item : 



Thank you,

turns.body_html: Production Orders showing red message that Vendor Lead time is greater than delivery date (but it is not) for vendors when trying to place an order for Northwest Early College for 9/1/25 - 9/5/25:

turns.author: agent:22001856315
turns.body_text: The warehouse only delivers on Mondays - 11-day lead time means the next available delivery is 9/1, which is a holiday, so it can't deliver that day.  Segovia only delivers on Fridays and the 29th is a holiday, so it can't deliver that day... the Friday before is the 22nd, which is less than 7 days away from now. Labatt should allow for 8/28 delivery - not sure why it's not, unless there is an item with a specific ordering lead time tied to an item?
turns.body_html: The warehouse only delivers on Mondays - 11-day lead time means the next available delivery is 9/1, which is a holiday, so it can't deliver that day.  Segovia only delivers on Fridays and the 29th is a holiday, so it can't deliver that day... the Friday before is the 22nd, which is less than 7 days away from now. Labatt should allow for 8/28 delivery - not sure why it's not, unless there is an item with a specific ordering lead time tied to an item?

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: Segovia's Distributing had a lead time of 7 days. The order was checked on 08/19, so it could not be delivered on 08/22/2025.  Central warehouse had a lead time of 11 days. The order was checked on 08/19, so it could not be delivered on 08/25/2025.  LABATT FOOD SERVICE has a lead time of 1 day. The order was checked on 08/19, so it should allow orders on 08/28/2025.     The inserted date range is 09/01 to 09/05. However, the system is displaying errors for dates before 09/01, which fall outside the specified range.

LABATT FOOD SERVICE has a lead time of 1 day. The order was checked on 08/19, so it should allow orders on 08/28/2025.

The inserted date range is 09/01 to 09/05. However, the system is displaying errors for dates before 09/01, which fall outside the specified range.

We have two schools that had issues with this last week. I thought it would correct itself this week, however it has happened again.

Gordons, Goldstar and Hilands are listed below…..Scarmardo is not even listed. We cannot figure out why.
This school was working last year, so not sure why it would disappear this year.
 
Thank you for investigating this…….Kevin

turns.body_html:
Hello Kala,

We have found a few causes of the issue: 

1) Enable "Display weight in the Recipe Steps". 


2) The Ingredient Is missing the serving measurements:


3) The Buying Guide is missing a lot of information.

subject: PBI 98800-Portland: Production order screen

turns.author: Linda Jue-Thomas <ljue@pps.net>
turns.body_text: Hello,   I am forwaring an issue reported to me. If you have additional questions, I can forward it on to someone who can answer it.     Apparently this is happening when teams are generating their orders through the "Production Orders" function. When the orders are generated for the schools to edit, the delivery dates are not sequential. It will go from 9/22, 9/23 skip to 9/26 followed by 9/24, 9/25 vs 9/22, 9/23, 9/24, 9/25, 9/26.      -Linda      --             Linda Jue-Thomas  ERP Systems Manager  Portland Public Schools | Nutrition Services  Phone: 503-916-3398
turns.body_html:
Hello,

I am forwaring an issue reported to me. If you have additional questions, I can forward it on to someone who can answer it.

Apparently this is happening when teams are generating their orders through the "Production Orders" function. When the orders are generated for the schools to edit, the delivery dates are not sequential. It will go from 9/22, 9/23 skip to 9/26 followed by 9/24, 9/25 vs 9/22, 9/23, 9/24, 9/25, 9/26. 

-Linda

--
Linda Jue-Thomas
ERP Systems Manager
Portland Public Schools | Nutrition Services
Phone: 503-916-3398

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Linda,     The display has the order numbers in sequential order... the orders get processed in the database, and the order numbers are applied as each order is created. The data is not always processed in "date" order...      If we were to make a change to the date order on this page, then the order numbers will not be sequential. As this page impacts many districts and users across the country, we are not planning on making changes to this page.      Order viewing typically takes place in the Inventory area of the software, where sorting and viewing can be performed differently.   ​               ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation
Guide and latest Release Notes in RESOURCES (formerly AMIGO)     Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click
Here     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket          Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Hello Linda,

The display has the order numbers in sequential order... the orders get processed in the database, and the order numbers are applied as each order is created. The data is not always processed in "date" order... 

If we were to make a change to the date order on this page, then the order numbers will not be sequential. As this page impacts many districts and users across the country, we are not planning on making changes to this page. 

Order viewing typically takes place in the Inventory area of the software, where sorting and viewing can be performed differently. 
​

Hello,

I am not sure why the delivery dates would not be in sequential orders. Is there a reason the system would not assign orders based on the delivery date? Is this a recent phenomenon? As this was not always the case.

Our teams order for a week at a time through the production orders. Having the dates not in order is making it challenging for our teams when it comes to placing and editing orders.

Thank you,
Ben

turns.body_html:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Hello,

I am not sure why the delivery dates would not be in sequential orders. Is there a reason the system would not assign orders based on the delivery date? Is this a recent phenomenon? As this was not always the case. 

Our teams order for a week at a time through the production orders. Having the dates not in order is making it challenging for our teams when it comes to placing and editing orders.

Thank you,
Ben
Ben Dandeneau
Director |Nutrition Services
Portland Public Schools
Office: 503-916-3275 | Cell: 971-480-2422


On Thu, Sep 11, 2025 at 2:46 PM Linda Jue-Thomas <ljue@pps.net> wrote:
My director, Ben, has some feedback to provide on this ticket so I am adding him to my reply so he can 'reply all' when he has a moment.

On Thu, Sep 11, 2025 at 8:56 AM customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello Linda,

The display has the order numbers in sequential order... the orders get processed in the database, and the order numbers are applied as each order is created. The data is not always processed in "date" order... 

If we were to make a change to the date order on this page, then the order numbers will not be sequential. As this page impacts many districts and users across the country, we are not planning on making changes to this page. 

Order viewing typically takes place in the Inventory area of the software, where sorting and viewing can be performed differently.

Hello Ben,  ​   The software processes the orders and they complete in the background... some orders may get processed faster than others due to the amount of data included in those orders, and are therefore not necessarily completed in "date order"... which means the order numbers are assigned as they complete.      We will be adding the ability to click the column headers so that you can sort them the way you want... the display will remain the way it is set up, and if you want the date column to be sorted instead of the order number, you'll have to click on that column header.     ​Please watch the release notes for that change to be released on a future version.           ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

turns.body_html:
Hello Ben,
​
The software processes the orders and they complete in the background... some orders may get processed faster than others due to the amount of data included in those orders, and are therefore not necessarily completed in "date order"... which means the order numbers are assigned as they complete.

We will be adding the ability to click the column headers so that you can sort them the way you want... the display will remain the way it is set up, and if you want the date column to be sorted instead of the order number, you'll have to click on that column header.

​Please watch the release notes for that change to be released on a future version.

The only order this item is on is the order expected on 9/11. I run the production order report after the managers submit their orders and the are pending for review. The only thing that comes to mind differently about this order is that it was modified by one of our administrators in the office. When an administrator places an order, they do not go in the pending for review category. They are open.

Does the production order reflect everything on the order submitted by an administrator? Only if orders are pending for review are they not counted in the production order?

Thanks,
Debra

subject: South Washington County Schools - Assist with Lead Times

turns.author: Mary Rasmussen <mrasmuss1@sowashco.org>
turns.body_text: .     Good morning,  I have a couple of questions about lead time and Production order usage dates.     We would like to be able to use M-F production usage dates and receive orders on Tuesday and Friday.     For example, if we are placing an order for usage dates of 9/29 through 10/3, we would like to get the 2 orders on Tuesday, 9/23 and Friday 9/26. However, it is giving us one order on Friday 9/26 and one order on Tuesday 9/30.  I have the vendor lead time and the production order lead time set at 2 days.     Anyway, I’m hoping someone can give me a call so that I can make sure this is set up correctly.     Thank you!          Mary Rasmussen  Account Specialist  Nutrition Services  Pronoun: she/her/hers     South Washington County Schools  mrasmuss1@sowashco.org   P: 651-425-6284   C: 612-508-0494.   F: 651-425-6312  sowashco.org  7362 East Point Douglas Road South Cottage Grove, MN 55016    COTTAGE GROVE  |  NEWPORT  |  ST. We are trying to set up IFD Food Service (IFD).

Both the vendor lead time and production lead time are currently set at 2 days.

Thanks for your help! ?

turns.body_text: The district is unable to place a Production Order for Labatt for delivery on 10/8/25 though PQOH shows the delivery availability for 10/8 and the Vendor Lead time is set to 2 days for Production orders and the district wants to know why this is.  It does seem that they can place an inventory order  for this delivery date though not a production order      The site is Hartman Elementary   Vendor Labatt  Delivery date 10/8/2025

turns.author: agent:22052027946
turns.body_text: The vendor has a production order delivery lead time of 2 days, with deliveries scheduled for Wednesdays and Thursdays. Ideally, the customer should be able to place an order for 10-08 (Wednesday). Attempted to create an order in UAT as well, but encountered the same issue there.           Even when I select 10-15 (Wednesday) to place the order and choose Labatts as the vendor, there is no option to add items.           Vendor information       No changes were made recently to vendor information.
turns.body_html:
The vendor has a production order delivery lead time of 2 days, with deliveries scheduled for Wednesdays and Thursdays. Ideally, the customer should be able to place an order for 10-08 (Wednesday). Attempted to create an order in UAT as well, but encountered the same issue there. 

Even when I select 10-15 (Wednesday) to place the order and choose Labatts as the vendor, there is no option to add items. 


Vendor information

No changes were made recently to vendor information.

turns.author: agent:22003349708
turns.body_text: They want the item on 10/8. 2 Day production lead time means they need it on 10/6. That is a Monday so it can't be delivered on that day since they only do Wednesday and Thursday. So they need it to arrive on Thursday 10/2. Then there is a 1 day vendor lead time which means they need to order on 10/1.
turns.body_html: They want the item on 10/8. 2 Day production lead time means they need it on 10/6. That is a Monday so it can't be delivered on that day since they only do Wednesday and Thursday. So they need it to arrive on Thursday 10/2. Then there is a 1 day vendor lead time which means they need to order on 10/1.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello ,     When you want the item to be received on 10/8, and because you have 2 Day production lead time on vendor configuration, which means you need it received on 10/6.       And 10/6 is a Monday. So it can't be delivered on that day since you have only Wednesday and Thursday for Delivery Schedule.      Hence, you will need it to arrive on a Thursday which was 10/2 and then there is a 1 day vendor lead time which means you should have placed and order on 10/1, but because you tried to place an order on 10/2, you got that warning.   ​   ​   Vendor information:                       ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

turns.body_html:
Hello ,

When you want the item to be received on 10/8, and because you have 2 Day production lead time on vendor configuration, which means you need it received on 10/6.  

And 10/6 is a Monday. So it can't be delivered on that day since you have only Wednesday and Thursday for Delivery Schedule. 

Hence, you will need it to arrive on a Thursday which was 10/2 and then there is a 1 day vendor lead time which means you should have placed and order on 10/1, but because you tried to place an order on 10/2, you got that warning.

CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Good morning,
I think this might be a setting issue, but I am not sure.  We are having trouble placing production orders for after fall break. It keeps picking up a delivery date request for today  even though we have the dates set as 10/20-10/23.  We have fall break next week, so we are not ordering for dates next week. We are supposed to be receiving our next delivery on 10/16.  How do we skip todays scheduled delivery in production order? Or should this week just be entered manually?
Jennifer Odom
Child Nutrition Services
Alvin Independent School District
281-245-3095

On Thu, Oct 9 at 8:59 AM , Jean Tubbs <jtubbs@alvinisd.net> wrote:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
That may have been unclear.  She already placed an order for the 10/9 delivery date which has been received and closed.  

On Thu, Oct 9, 2025 at 8:45 AM Jean Tubbs <jtubbs@alvinisd.net> wrote:
The school in question placed a Hiland Dairy order on 10/9 which is closed.

On Thu, Oct 9, 2025 at 8:43 AM Jennifer Odom Wemhoff <jodom@alvinisd.net> wrote:
We don't necessarily want to skip deliveries during holidays because we sometimes get deliveries during our breaks, but they just don't need a delivery right before the break because the food will go bad over the break. The vendor delivery lead time for DOD is 1 day. It sounds to me like they will just need to do manual orders and not production orders for these dates like this? 
Jennifer Odom
Child Nutrition Services
Alvin Independent School District
281-245-3095
- a flower does not think of competing with the flower next to it, it just blooms- iris murdoch



On Thu, Oct 9, 2025 at 8:30 AM customercare@primeroedge.com <customercare@primeroedge.com> wrote:
Hello Jennifer,

There are many things involved in the answer to that question, unfortunately. It encompasses whether deliveries were skipped during the holiday time, what the lead time is for that vendor as well as the delivery schedule for the site or the warehouse - it is going to give you the delivery date that it can give based on a combination of everything you have set. Hopefully that make sense.

Good morning,
I think this might be a setting issue, but I am not sure.  We are having trouble placing production orders for after fall break. It keeps picking up a delivery date request for today  even though we have the dates set as 10/20-10/23.  We have fall break next week, so we are not ordering for dates next week. We are supposed to be receiving our next delivery on 10/16.  How do we skip todays scheduled delivery in production order? Or should this week just be entered manually?
Jennifer Odom
Child Nutrition Services
Alvin Independent School District
281-245-3095

turns.body_html:
Escalating for review.  Site Independent Elementary created order for Central warehouse on dates 11/3-11/10 but it only generated 1 order where they were expecting 2 and the single order does not appear to contain all items expected

  • Order # 43919 does not appear to contain items expected for 11/5 delivery.
  • Example Item: 410433 (Beef, Steak, Philly), used in Recipe LR1600 (Beef and Broccoli and Rice).
  • Site Independent Elementary has 0 QOH of 410433
  • Plan for 11/6 Thu contains QTY 100
  • PQOH orders seems to indicate that a qty would be needed for 11/5 delivery
  • Even if you try to select start 11/5 to 11/10 it advises order already generated
NOTE: Site is supposed to have order submitted to warehouse by 12pm PST (2pm CST monday). Sebira's contact is: 510.537.3000 ext 7013 for questions or if any issues.

Reference Images:

turns.body_html:
Please take a look at ticket #303473 raised by Kristi Wiseman (kwiseman@usd259.net).
​
Hi Michael, 

But you have looed at the Delivery date of 11/25/25 for the Items required on 12/1/2025 production. 

But the issue reported is with the Delivery date of 12/1/2025. The Order that was supposed to get created with Delivery date of 12/1/2025 has not been created when they Placed Production orders, but when they are looking at the PQOH orders, it then shows the Items that will be delivering on 12/1/2025. 

The Question is when there are clearly items that needs to get delivered on 12/1/25 showing on PQOH, why did Production Orders not create an order for that delivery date. 

Thank you,

turns.author: agent:22003349708
turns.body_text: PQOH operates differently then normal production orders screen. PQOH is designed to use the current inventory state to order what is coming up. Production orders looks at records within a date range and creates an order based on the current state and then no longer changes. PQOH can in theory be run every day and have orders generated for the same date if the usage or perpetual changes. PQOH requires the perpetual be kept up to date.

turns.author: agent:22004298836
turns.body_text: According to the report on the production order page, Hopkins bananas were ordered for delivery on 1/20 - and I do see them on order PE26712       for Sandburg - the honeydew and cantaloupe are showing as "Exceptions" on the report on the production orders page, and when I look at the products, they are "Receivable only" for Sandburg (And Euclid), which means they are not going to propagate on an order. If they need to order them - the district needs to set the items as Orderable.

subject: Mabank ISD - Grocery List to Production Orders

turns.author: Brittani Frankel <brittani.frankel@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: The Mabank school district called this morning and was wondering if there was a way to utilize the Grocery List and transfer the information to the Production Orders without having to go back through and enter each number.      I asked Chris R., as that is a new question for me, and he said that the Production Orders are basically like the Grocery List, but simpler, as they already have an idea of how much of each item they believe you may need.      That didn't exactly answer the question at hand... I told the district that, based on the answer I was given, I believe the answer is no.         Production > Plan and Record > Production Orders and Grocery List
turns.body_html:
The Mabank school district called this morning and was wondering if there was a way to utilize the Grocery List and transfer the information to the Production Orders without having to go back through and enter each number. 

I asked Chris R., as that is a new question for me, and he said that the Production Orders are basically like the Grocery List, but simpler, as they already have an idea of how much of each item they believe you may need. 

That didn't exactly answer the question at hand... I told the district that, based on the answer I was given, I believe the answer is no. 


Production > Plan and Record > Production Orders and Grocery List

subject: Moreno Valley Unified School District / Editing orders

turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
 
 
 
 
 Good afternoon,  
   
 I have a question regarding production orders. We currently have Labatt Food Service and Labatt Produce set up as 2 different vendors due to our ordering cycle and production order lead time. For Labatt Food Service, we order 2 weeks ahead,
 and for Labatt Produce, we order 1 week ahead.  
   
 Our order guide is set up as follows:  
 Labatt Food Service: Order due on 1/20: Production date 2/5-2/11 
 Labatt Produce: Order due on 1/20: Production date 1/29-2/4 
   
 Our vendor settings are as follows:  
 Labatt Food Service: Vendor Lead Time: 5 days; Production Order Delivery Lead Time: 2 days
 
 Labatt Produce: Vendor Lead Time: 2 days; Production Order Delivery Lead Time: 1 day 
 We shortened the production order delivery lead time for Labatt Produce to allow produce to be delivered on Tuesday to serve on Wednesday. 
   
 Attached is a production order created by Paetow High School. “Paetow Production Order 1” was created on 1/12 because on 1/12, her Labatt Food Service order is due for production date 1/29-2/4. “Paetow Production Order 2” was created earlier
 today for production dates 1/29-2/4. When we generate production orders, the production orders are generated for all vendors.
 
   
 Is there a setting for us to not allow the merging of orders at the point of order generation? If not, what suggestions do you have to prevent ordering issues if the items are delivered by the same vendor but separated in Primero due to
 production order lead time?     
   
 
 
 
 Best Regards,
 
   
 Thao Vo, MS, RD, LD  
 Associate Director of Policy and Procurement 
 Katy Independent School District  
   
 Nutrition & Food Service  
 5364 Franz Road, Building B 
 Katy, TX 77493 
 
Office: 281-396-6240  
 
Fax: 281-644-1815  
 
E-mail: 
thaovo@katyisd.org

Good afternoon,
 
I have a question regarding production orders. We currently have Labatt Food Service and Labatt Produce set up as 2 different vendors due to our ordering cycle and production order lead time. For Labatt Food Service, we order 2 weeks ahead, and for Labatt Produce, we order 1 week ahead.
 
Our order guide is set up as follows:
Labatt Food Service: Order due on 1/20: Production date 2/5-2/11
Labatt Produce: Order due on 1/20: Production date 1/29-2/4
 
Our vendor settings are as follows:
Labatt Food Service: Vendor Lead Time: 5 days; Production Order Delivery Lead Time: 2 days
Labatt Produce: Vendor Lead Time: 2 days; Production Order Delivery Lead Time: 1 day
We shortened the production order delivery lead time for Labatt Produce to allow produce to be delivered on Tuesday to serve on Wednesday.
 
Attached is a production order created by Paetow High School. “Paetow Production Order 1” was created on 1/12 because on 1/12, her Labatt Food Service order is due for production date 1/29-2/4. “Paetow Production Order 2” was created earlier today for production dates 1/29-2/4. When we generate production orders, the production orders are generated for all vendors.
 
Is there a setting for us to not allow the merging of orders at the point of order generation? If not, what suggestions do you have to prevent ordering issues if the items are delivered by the same vendor but separated in Primero due to production order lead time?    
 
Best Regards,
 
Thao Vo, MS, RD, LD

turns.body_text: This is what AI says but I have to confirm if this is the case somehow.      the distribution has not yet been consolidated. In PrimeroEdge, the Production Plan and subsequent production stages are driven by consolidated orders from the sites to the central warehouse.     Why Cancelled Orders Are Blocking the Process    If the orders for 2/9–2/13 were cancelled, the system currently sees zero active demand for those dates. Since the Production Plan is built from "counts of finished goods that result from production orders placed by the sites," no items will appear on the production calendar if there are no active (Open) orders to consolidate.     Cancelled orders cannot be processed in receipts because the system has already recorded a "Reversal" transaction. Only orders with an Open status can be moved through the receipting and distribution workflow.      Since the previous batch was cancelled, the sites may need to place new orders for the week of 2/9–2/13.   Once the sites submit new orders, they will appear on your Distribution Calendar for those dates.  Because you have updated the Delivery Route, these new orders will automatically be assigned to the updated route and delivery schedule you configured.   Before attempting to move to production, ensure the new orders are listed correctly:   Navigate to Inventory > Central Warehouse > Distribution.  Select the dates (2/9–2/13) on the calendar.  The calendar status for those dates should be Not Consolidated (typically represented by an orange or white color).  Confirm that the new orders appear in the Orders section at the bottom of the screen with a status of Open.   You must consolidate the orders to push them into the Central Production module.   On the Distribution screen for the selected dates, click the Consolidate Orders button.  A green message will appear confirming that the orders have been consolidated and a Distribution # has been assigned.  Note: Once this is done, the items will finally appear in the Central Production > Production Plan for your production team to begin work.     If the sites are unable to re-place their orders because the system says an "order already exists" for those dates:   Click View Order Exceptions on the Distribution screen to see which sites are missing active orders.  If the cancelled orders are still appearing as a block, you may need to Delete the specific cancelled order rows if your role permissions allow, or have the sites check their "Saved" orders list to ensure no drafts are conflicting.   Summary of Workflow to Move Forward   Sites: Place new orders for 2/9–2/13.  Warehouse: Go to Distribution, select dates, and click Consolidate Orders.  Production: Go to Central Production > Production Plan; the items will now be visible for processing.
turns.body_html:
This is what AI says but I have to confirm if this is the case somehow.

 the distribution has not yet been consolidated. In PrimeroEdge, the Production Plan and subsequent production stages are driven by consolidated orders from the sites to the central warehouse.

Why Cancelled Orders Are Blocking the Process
  1.  If the orders for 2/9–2/13 were cancelled, the system currently sees zero active demand for those dates. Since the Production Plan is built from "counts of finished goods that result from production orders placed by the sites," no items will appear on the production calendar if there are no active (Open) orders to consolidate.

  2.  Cancelled orders cannot be processed in receipts because the system has already recorded a "Reversal" transaction. Only orders with an Open status can be moved through the receipting and distribution workflow.

Since the previous batch was cancelled, the sites may need to place new orders for the week of 2/9–2/13.
  • Once the sites submit new orders, they will appear on your Distribution Calendar for those dates.
  • Because you have updated the Delivery Route, these new orders will automatically be assigned to the updated route and delivery schedule you configured.
Before attempting to move to production, ensure the new orders are listed correctly:
  • Navigate to Inventory > Central Warehouse > Distribution.
  • Select the dates (2/9–2/13) on the calendar.
  • The calendar status for those dates should be Not Consolidated (typically represented by an orange or white color).
  • Confirm that the new orders appear in the Orders section at the bottom of the screen with a status of Open.
You must consolidate the orders to push them into the Central Production module.
  • On the Distribution screen for the selected dates, click the Consolidate Orders button.
  • A green message will appear confirming that the orders have been consolidated and a Distribution # has been assigned.
  • Note: Once this is done, the items will finally appear in the Central Production > Production Plan for your production team to begin work.

If the sites are unable to re-place their orders because the system says an "order already exists" for those dates:
  • Click View Order Exceptions on the Distribution screen to see which sites are missing active orders.
  • If the cancelled orders are still appearing as a block, you may need to Delete the specific cancelled order rows if your role permissions allow, or have the sites check their "Saved" orders list to ensure no drafts are conflicting.
Summary of Workflow to Move Forward
  1. Sites: Place new orders for 2/9–2/13.
  2. Warehouse: Go to Distribution, select dates, and click Consolidate Orders.
  3. Production: Go to Central Production > Production Plan; the items will now be visible for processing.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello ,     ​Item 4235100  is set up as an interchangeable substitute of item 60324.  ​Because 60324 is a lesser price than 4235100, the software is working as designed by allowing users to obtain the cheaper item.  ​The cheaper item is not showing up in production orders, as it is marked as "Receivable only."  ​   ​If these items are not interchangeable, then that substitute setting needs to be modified.  (If the substitution should not be occurring on orders, and should only be a warehouse designation, then removing the "interchangeable" setting will disallow the usage of this secondary product on Production Orders).  ​   ​If the sites should be able to order the substitute item, then item 60324 should be set as orderable.      ​​   ​   Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here     Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket          Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Hello ,

​Item 4235100  is set up as an interchangeable substitute of item 60324.
​Because 60324 is a lesser price than 4235100, the software is working as designed by allowing users to obtain the cheaper item.
​The cheaper item is not showing up in production orders, as it is marked as "Receivable only."
​
​If these items are not interchangeable, then that substitute setting needs to be modified.
(If the substitution should not be occurring on orders, and should only be a warehouse designation, then removing the "interchangeable" setting will disallow the usage of this secondary product on Production Orders).
​
​If the sites should be able to order the substitute item, then item 60324 should be set as orderable. 

​​
​

Hello Heidi,
 
Please read the full details of this email.  The additional information we are needing or providing is located towards the bottom in the information needed or provided section.  

This automated email is to let you know that we are awaiting your response from you on the following ticket: 
 
Ticket Number: 310056
Subject: Belton ISD - Stock item not pulling into production orders

Kindly review the additional clarification given or reply back with the necessary requested information below so we can continue to work on the issue. 
Information needed or provided:
Schrenia Bockholt :
Hello ,

​Item 4235100  is set up as an interchangeable substitute of item 60324.
​Because 60324 is a lesser price than 4235100, the software is working as designed by allowing users to obtain the cheaper item.
​The cheaper item is not showing up in production orders, as it is marked as 'Receivable only.'
​
​If these items are not interchangeable, then that substitute setting needs to be modified.
(If the substitution should not be occurring on orders, and should only be a warehouse designation, then removing the 'interchangeable' setting will disallow the usage of this secondary product on Production Orders).
​
​If the sites should be able to order the substitute item, then item 60324 should be set as orderable. 

​​
​

Hello Shelby,     Thank you for your patience in review. To quickly summarize, I believe you should be able to either add a M-F day to the vendor delivery schedule OR change the Holiday calendar for Sunday weekends to allow delivery and then the Vendor should show.     This appears related to the Vendor Delivery date and Holiday calendar weekends. Currently the vendor is setup for delivering on Sundays:       The Sites are setup to order directly from the vendor (rather than say from the central warehouse) - reference example item 21834 Apples, Sliced:       If I go to the Holiday Calendary schedule under Menu Planning > Configuration >Holiday Schedule >Show Weekends > navigate to 3/1 and 3/8 >expand selection - it shows sites are not setup on the weekends to allow deliveries:       Testing to confirm in the UAT as well, if I either added Monday as a delivery day to the Vendor configuration OR updated the Holiday schedule, then the vendor(s) would show on PQOH or Production orders see screenshot below:           At this time, I cannot say specifically what or when a change occurred affecting this but as it was also showing like this in the UAT it would seem sometime before 2/20 when the data was refreshed on the UAT. Hello Shelby,

Thank you for your patience in review. To quickly summarize, I believe you should be able to either add a M-F day to the vendor delivery schedule OR change the Holiday calendar for Sunday weekends to allow delivery and then the Vendor should show.

This appears related to the Vendor Delivery date and Holiday calendar weekends. Currently the vendor is setup for delivering on Sundays:

The Sites are setup to order directly from the vendor (rather than say from the central warehouse) - reference example item 21834 Apples, Sliced:

If I go to the Holiday Calendary schedule under Menu Planning > Configuration >Holiday Schedule >Show Weekends > navigate to 3/1 and 3/8 >expand selection - it shows sites are not setup on the weekends to allow deliveries:

Testing to confirm in the UAT as well, if I either added Monday as a delivery day to the Vendor configuration OR updated the Holiday schedule, then the vendor(s) would show on PQOH or Production orders see screenshot below:

At this time, I cannot say specifically what or when a change occurred affecting this but as it was also showing like this in the UAT it would seem sometime before 2/20 when the data was refreshed on the UAT.

turns.body_html:
Hello Shelby,

I called a moment ago but did not reach you and left a voicemail. I should be available for a quick call after 2:15pm if that should help. I'm not certain I am understanding the question correctly.

I believe you are meaning you would like the sites to be able to accept delivery on Sunday, correct?  For this you would want to make the change under the Holiday schedule. You would have to click on each weekend day to allow delivery for sites.... I'm not familiar with a mass way to select "every sunday" so would have to inquire further on this (in the short run you should be able to adjust just 3/1 and 3/8 under Menu Planning > Configuration >Holiday schedule - see prior screenshot).  

With only CC Produce and Roma having "Sunday" selected on their vendor setup under Inventory>Configuration>Vendor > select vendor > click Site Delivery Setting, only those would show up (2 settings working together). Also if you meant adding a different day to CC Produce or Roma  only, that is controlled here too see following screenshot

turns.author: agent:22004661029
turns.body_text: The site cannot use both PQOH order and Production orders functionality. They should only be using either of them.   They fundamentally behave differently so it doesn't make sense to use both.      This is as per the current design of the system.
turns.body_html:
The site cannot use both PQOH order and Production orders functionality. They should only be using either of them. 
They fundamentally behave differently so it doesn't make sense to use both.

This is as per the current design of the system.

Hello,     Since this ticket was created yesterday on 3/11, and you were trying to create a production order with a delivery date of 3/11, this would not go through. You cannot have an order delivered on the same day in which you'd created the order. In addition, the one-day lead time that the vendor has set would also preclude that from occurring in the software.     We're attempting to recreate the issue in the website, but are unable to replicate it. Can you let us know the steps you're trying to take that are causing the error to occur? turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Harriet,     I totally forgot that when placing production orders the system will look for any interchangeable items as well.  The applesauce cups have seven interchangeable items and since the site has enough product on hand (including these interchangeable items) the production orders will not add anymore since they have enough  on hand:               Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by

turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.
Hi, Terri:

Production orders are ordering templates breaks down directly from the plan numbers, recipes in the specified date/time range and shouldn't consider the current QOH or interchangeable items. This is due to managers are ordering two weeks far out. The QOH numbers are covering the current week's demand so I don't think the system should even consider QOH at this point.

Our managers are using production orders and the orders should only round up the grocery list's demanded quantities, not taking consideration of the current QOH at all. Based on the suggestions, managers will modify/delete/add whatever they need on top of it.

Is there any reason that burger is gone too? From our experience, the production order should be equal to the grocery list item list with rounded-up whole numbers. Please let us know the logic of the missing items. Thank you so much for your assistance.

Harriet Chiang
Compliance and Technology Specialist
PISD Food and Nutritional Services
6600 Alma Drive, Suite B
Plano, TX  75023
Office: 469-752-5789

Subject: RE: Online 3 - Plano ISD - Missing Items in Production Order-[#314213]
 
Hi, Terri:
 
Production orders are ordering templates breaks down directly from the plan numbers, recipes in the specified date/time range and shouldn’t consider the current QOH or interchangeable items. This is due to managers are ordering two weeks far out. The QOH numbers are covering the current week’s demand so I don’t think the system should even consider QOH at this point.
 
Our managers are using production orders and the orders should only round up the grocery list’s demanded quantities, not taking consideration of the current QOH at all. Based on the suggestions, managers will modify/delete/add whatever they need on top of it.
 
Is there any reason that burger is gone too? From our experience, the production order should be equal to the grocery list item list with rounded-up whole numbers. Please let us know the logic of the missing items. Thank you so much for your assistance.
 
Harriet Chiang

Jade Kelley
Hi, can I please have some assistance with production orders?
04:40 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Thank you for contacting customer care. I hope you're having a great day!
04:50 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
What help are you needing
04:50 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
okay, I'm viewing a production order for Twin Oaks Elementary
05:01 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
4/16-4/22
05:01 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
Under the vendor Hollandia dairy
05:01 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
05:01 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
what does a number like 1 , 47.6614 actually mean?
05:02 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
I'm trying to become more familiar with production orders because only 1 of our sites actually utilizes it and I'd like for them to all start using it
05:03 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Sorry for the delay, I had to handle something from this side
05:16 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
So in that case, that 3rd column is your expected usage for the week by whole and broken units
05:18 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
So, I would read it as 1 case & 47.6614 broken units?
05:19 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Correct
05:21 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
do you know why it's not a whole number?
05:21 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Let me check the item and see
05:22 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
okay
05:22 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
It looks like it is in how the item is set up, it is trying to calculate based on something related to the ingredient
05:30 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Do you use fractions or decimals for other items? I know the option to even use decimals is a system setting, and if you would prefer it to round and not use decimals for anything that can be achieved
05:31 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Most things are not ordered in decimals, so most people turn it off all together unless they do order things in those amounts
05:32 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
Are there any potential negative drawbacks to not having the decimals?
05:32 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Only if it would ever be required to order in decimals for any item
05:34 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Not saying this is true in your case per se, but maybe there is a some item that you can order or receive half a bag or something for
05:35 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
Oh I see
05:43 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
okay, I will ask my Director
05:51 PM, 15th Apr
Jade Kelley
thank you for your help
05:51 PM, 15th Apr
Jared
Let me know, if we need to fix the item I can help you to do so tomorrow
05:54 PM, 15th Apr

subject: Online 3 - Boulder Valley School District - Inventory reports

turns.author: Hillary Hecht <hillary.hecht@bvsd.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Hi Terri,

The other day, we ran out of smoothies when we thought we had ordered enough.

I am wondering what reports you would suggest running to determine if we have enough inventory to make the menu items we need for the week, and determine if we have enough to fulfill the school orders that we need to ship out.

Thanks,

Hillary Hecht, CN
Food Services Technician
Boulder Valley School District
(720) 561-2584
Cell - 303-506-7674

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:

subject: Argyle ISD- Question about production order

turns.author: Amy Godwin <amy.godwin@argyleisd.com>
turns.body_text: Amy called with questions about production orders.
turns.body_html: Amy called with questions about production orders.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text: Question: Customer states that more items should be populating for Scott Gibson Middle School             A number of items listed on menu plans, such as P600188, are included in these menus (the vendor is the same that is listed in the order) and don't populate on the Orders.        Should these items (example P600188 ) appear in the order? I'm not sure how the system auto populates items in Production orders.
turns.body_html:
Question: Customer states that more items should be populating for Scott Gibson Middle School



A number of items listed on menu plans, such as P600188, are included in these menus (the vendor is the same that is listed in the order) and don't populate on the Orders. 



Should these items (example P600188 ) appear in the order? I'm not sure how the system auto populates items in Production orders.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Good morning Amy,     Is Scott Gibson a new school in the district? I'm finding that products are not set up to be orderable for them.   I selected a single menu item of Taco, Bacon, Egg & Cheese (LR1879) - and in reviewing the linked stock items, they are not orderable.      The easiest way to fix this, would be to go to Inventory > Configuration > Site Order Guide Set Up  From there, you can select "Configured Site" (which appears to be Argyle Middle School) Then in the "Destination Site" - select "Scott Gibson" - and then click the button labeled "Copy"  This will set up Scott Gibson to have the same access to items that Argyle Middle does, and then the orders will function as expected.                  Check out PrimeroEdge Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here  SchoolCafe Training WEBINARS Available: Click Here    Let us know if you have any questions regarding this ticket by
replying all to this ticket         Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Good morning Amy,

Is Scott Gibson a new school in the district? I'm finding that products are not set up to be orderable for them. 
I selected a single menu item of Taco, Bacon, Egg & Cheese (LR1879) - and in reviewing the linked stock items, they are not orderable. 

The easiest way to fix this, would be to go to Inventory > Configuration > Site Order Guide Set Up
From there, you can select "Configured Site" (which appears to be Argyle Middle School)
Then in the "Destination Site" - select "Scott Gibson" - and then click the button labeled "Copy"
This will set up Scott Gibson to have the same access to items that Argyle Middle does, and then the orders will function as expected.
